---
title: "implicit_least_squares"
generated: true
description: "Differentiable argmin of a least-squares residual by the implicit theorem."
---

[‚Äπ back to `nitrix.linalg.optimize`](../nitrix-linalg-optimize.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:implicit_least_squares -->
```python
implicit_least_squares(residual_fn: DataResidualFn, data: Any, x0: Float[Array, ' p'], *, n_iters: int = 20, init_lambda: float = 0.001, cg_tol: float = 1e-06, cg_maxiter: Optional[int] = None) -> Float[Array, ' p']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:implicit_least_squares -->
Differentiable argmin of a least-squares residual by the implicit
theorem.

Compute the argmin of $\tfrac{1}{2}\lVert r(\mathrm{data}, x)
\rVert^2$, differentiable with respect to `data` by the
**implicit-function theorem** rather than by unrolling the solver.

The forward solve is Levenberg-Marquardt; the backward differentiates
through the *converged* $x^*$ directly.  At the optimum the
stationarity $g(\mathrm{data}, x^*) = J^{\top}r = 0$ gives, by the
implicit-function theorem,
$\partial x^* / \partial \mathrm{data} = -(J^{\top}J)^{-1}
\partial_{\mathrm{data}} g$ (Gauss-Newton Hessian -- exact when the
residual is small or the model is linear, the standard least-squares
implicit-diff approximation).  The backward solves
$(J^{\top}J)\, w = \bar{x}$ with [`cg`](../nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd) (SPD, matrix-free --
stays on-device) and pushes $-w$ back through
$\partial_{\mathrm{data}} g$.  Memory is $O(1)$ in the
iteration count (vs unrolling's $O(n\_iters)$) and
$O(M + P)$ per call: neither the $M \times P$ Jacobian nor the
$P \times P$ normal matrix is materialised -- [`cg`](../nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd) holds
$O(P)$ iterates and the linearisation caches one residual evaluation
($O(M)$ voxels), independent of $P^2$.

This is the differentiable-layer entry point: a registration whose
residual is
$r(\mathrm{images}, \theta) = \mathrm{warp}(\mathrm{moving},
\theta) - \mathrm{fixed}$ becomes differentiable with respect to the
images by calling this with `data = (moving, fixed)` (the unrolled
recipe is the always-available alternative).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:implicit_least_squares -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `residual_fn` | `DataResidualFn` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | Map `(data, x) -> r` (`data` is the differentiable argument, an array or pytree; `x` is the optimisation variable, `(P,)`, mapping to a residual `(M,)`). |
| `data` | `Any` | ‚Äî | ‚Äî | The differentiable parameters / inputs (an array or pytree). |
| `x0` | `Float` | `p` | ‚Äî | Initial guess for `x`, `(P,)`. |
| `n_iters` | `int` | ‚Äî | `20` | Forward Levenberg-Marquardt controls. |
| `init_lambda` | `float` | ‚Äî | `0.001` | Forward Levenberg-Marquardt controls. |
| `cg_tol` | `float` | ‚Äî | `1e-06` | Backward adjoint-solve ([`cg`](../nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd)) controls. |
| `cg_maxiter` | `Optional[int]` | ‚Äî | `None` | Backward adjoint-solve ([`cg`](../nitrix-linalg-krylov/cg.qmd)) controls. |

**Returns** `Float` ‚Äî shape `p`. The minimiser $x^*$, `(P,)`. $\mathrm{d}x^* / \mathrm{d}x_0 = 0$ (the initial guess does not affect the gradient).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:implicit_least_squares -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
